text: When a smoothed Burgers bore (u: descending step, amplitude 1.5) meets a KdV-type sech^2 soliton (v: amp 1.5 at x=-8) under the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system on x in [-15,15], does v survive (peak >= 0.5) and u stay bounded (|u_max|<5) at T=8? Identify whether the soliton transmits, reflects, fuses, or is destroyed.

method: Fourier pseudospectral derivatives (no dealiasing, no filter) + classical explicit RK4 over full RHS (v_xxx treated explicitly); periodic domain
bank_use_rationale: Progressive-complexity discipline NON-NEGOTIABLE: E1 must be simplest baseline (Fourier pseudospectral + explicit RK4, no dealiasing, no IMEX). Negative entries warn that (a) central FD on hyperbolic shock-forming u-Burgers (kb-burgers-fwdEuler-centralFD-Gibbs, kb-general-centralFD-hyperbolic-shockFormation) produces Gibbs oscillations -- so I choose Fourier pseudospectral over central FD even at baseline since both are 'simplest' but spectral is fairer for v_xxx; (b) explicit RK4 on v_xxx (kb-kdv-explicit-RK4-stiffness-blowup) and no-dealiasing (kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts, BKdV-S1) cause aliasing/stiffness blow-up. I REJECT pre-emptive use of IMEX or dealiasing because protocol requires observing the failure mode first; bank entries are noted for E2 escalation direction.

diagnostics.blowup_signature: overflow encountered in multiply (then exponential field growth)

rationale: Baseline Fourier pseudospectral + RK4 with no dealiasing overflowed at t=0.56, well before T=8. Failure mode matches BKdV-S1: quadratic products (v^2, u*v, v*v_x) wrap aliased energy into resolved modes; combined with explicit RK4 on v_xxx, the high-k cascade exceeds floating-point range. v reached 1e91 -- not just spurious peaks but full numerical blow-up. Two single-component upgrades are suggested by bank: (a) add 2/3 dealiasing on quadratic products (kb-kdv-noDealiasing); (b) switch v_xxx to IMEX (kb-kdv-explicit-RK4-stiffness-blowup). Per progressive-complexity, E2 changes EXACTLY one.

BKdV-S1 deep synthesis says: at this Nx/dt/amp, the BINDING wall is broadband aliasing, NOT explicit-RK4 stiffness. R2 of S1 added only the 2/3-rule and reached T=10 cleanly at the SAME dt. So the prescribed single-component upgrade for E2 is: keep Fourier pseudospectral + RK4 + same dt, add 2/3-rule dealiasing on the nonlinear products (v^2, u*v, plus dealias state before nonlinear evaluation). Do NOT also switch to IMEX -- that would be two components at once.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing on nonlinear products and on state pre-nonlinear-eval + classical explicit RK4. Single-component change vs E1: add dealiasing mask |k_idx|>Nx/3 set to 0.
bank_use_rationale: Single-component upgrade vs E1: add 2/3-rule dealiasing. BKdV-S1 deep synthesis (depth=3) certified that at Nx=256, T<=10, amp<=3.0 the binding wall is broadband aliasing not stiffness; dealiasing alone reaches T=10 cleanly at the same dt=2e-4. I REJECT (a) simultaneously switching to IMEX/IFRK4 (kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup, kb-gardner-G4-IMEX-CN-amplitudeCFL-blowup -- known implementation risk and amplitude-CFL traps), (b) switching to upwind/MUSCL on u (kb-burgers-fwdEuler-centralFD-Gibbs -- relevant but spectral derivative of u is not central FD; Gibbs warning is moot for spectral u_x on the smoothed bore here), (c) Lax-Friedrichs (kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-longTime-dissipation -- excessive damping over T=8). Single change keeps the experiment debuggable.

rationale: Dealiasing fixed the blow-up; the system reached T=8 cleanly with mass conservation. v survives (peak 1.505 >= 0.5: target met). BUT |u_max|=13.8 violates the |u_max|<5 target -- u sharpened into a Gibbs-style overshoot at the bore as predicted by kb-general-centralFD-hyperbolic-shockFormation (spectral derivative behaves like central FD on a shock-forming u). v also shows 10 peaks (oscillatory ringing from u-coupling forcing into v through d/dx(uv)). Single component to upgrade: replace explicit-spectral treatment of the Burgers nonlinear u u_x with an upwind / Godunov / hyperviscosity-stabilised treatment for u (the bore-tracking component).

BKdV-S1 R3-extension says: 'push a steep Burgers-favoring IC so u*u_x steepens past the 2/3 band, then switch to MUSCL-Godunov on u*u_x'. kb-general-centralFD-hyperbolic-shockFormation: 'central FD without upwinding is catastrophic for any hyperbolic system with discontinuous ICs.' kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-overdiffusion warns against global LxF (smears bore ~28% more than HLL). BKdV-S5 used 'IMEX-CN on v_xxx + MUSCL-Godunov on 3 u u_x' as the stable stack. E3 single-component change vs E2: add a mild hyperviscosity (k^p damping) targeted at the high-k content of u to stabilise the bore -- this is the SIMPLEST single-component upgrade (one additional linear term) over MUSCL or HLL reconstruction which would be a larger architectural change. Hyperviscosity acts only on |k|>k_cut so it suppresses the Gibbs overshoot without artificially smearing physics. kb-shallowWater-LaxFriedrichs-overdiffusion is rejected; using HLL is two-component change. BKdV-S4 deep synthesis warns nu_h > 1e-20 reshapes the attractor -- so I will use weak nu_h in S4-validated range.

method: E2 stack (Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing + classical RK4) + weak hyperviscosity -nu_h * k^16 on both u and v (single new linear term per field). Explicit treatment (RK4 absorbs it).
bank_use_rationale: Single-component upgrade: add weak hyperviscosity nu_h*k^16 (BKdV-S4 validated this nu_h<=1e-22 envelope at Nx=256, T=10). I REJECT: (a) global Lax-Friedrichs (kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-longTime-dissipation: severely damps the bore over T=8>>shock timescale; kb-shallowWater-LaxFriedrichs-overdiffusion: smears 28%); (b) positivity-clip on u (kb-shallowWater-dryBed-naiveClip-hu-singular: clipping breaks conservation, produces hu/h singular ratios; not directly applicable here but same dishonest-stabilization principle); (c) IMEX-CN (kb-gardner-G4-IMEX-CN-amplitudeCFL-blowup, kb-gardner-cubicTerm-tightens-nonlinearCFL: at amp 1.5 the IMEX nonlinear-CFL is fine but coupling to the bore would change effective amplitude; introducing IMEX is a 2-component change since I would need to refactor the time stepper). BKdV-S4 deep synthesis warns nu_h>1e-20 actively reshapes the attractor -- I stay in the safe weak-HV regime. RK4 hyperviscous stability bound dt<=2/(nu_h*k_max^16)~0.13 at Nx=256, nu_h=1e-22; dt=1e-4 sits 3 orders below.

diagnostics.interpretation: u_max grows monotonically from t=0 onward; growth begins at t=1 BEFORE soliton meets bore (soliton at x=-8 reaches x=0 by t~4 at KdV speed v_kdv~2A=3). This indicates the bore self-steepens immediately under its own Burgers dynamics, with the v_xx source -d/dx(v_xx) also pumping u even at t<1 via the spectral high-k components.

rationale: E3 (weak HV nu_h=1e-22) marginally improved over E2 (|u_max| 13.8->11.0); both have v peak well above 0.5 (target met) but |u_max|>5 (target failed). The Burgers bore self-steepens; spectral derivatives produce Gibbs-style overshoot at the steep front of u. v survived with peak 1.599 -- the soliton transmits through (and partially fuses with) the bore, generating ~15 small oscillations downstream. Mass conservation is exact. The result is qualitatively correct (soliton transmits, bore intact and amplified, no NaN) but quantitatively violates |u_max|<5; addressing this would require either MUSCL/upwind on u (2-component change) or much stronger HV (would reshape attractor per BKdV-S4 warning).

3 iterations consumed. E3 is the best of the three. v peak target met (1.599 >> 0.5). |u_max|=11.02 violates the strict <5 target, but the field is bounded (no NaN, no overflow); the bore amplification is partly physical (PDE source d/dx(v_xx) pumps u). Going to E4 would require either an architectural change to MUSCL/upwind on the Burgers component or strong-HV (BKdV-S4 deep synthesis warns this reshapes the attractor). Submitting E3 as final answer; reasoning.md documents the partial outcome.
